entry.title: News Releases
entry.summary: Ames Lab will help advance AI-guided manufacturing of high-performance magnets and strengthen domestic supply chains

entry.title: Feature Stories
entry.summary: Researchers at the U. S. Department of Energy Ames National Laboratory are advancing materials research with the installation of a new high-field Dynamic Nuclear Polarization Nuclear Magnetic Resonance (DNP-NMR) spectrometer. The system strengthens the lab’s DNP-NMR research capabilities by providing a much higher resolution for solid-state NMR experiments.

entry.title: In the News
entry.summary: Partnership supports national effort to strengthen critical materials supply chains and manufacturing.

entry.title: Media Contacts
entry.summary: The Ames Laboratory Communications Office provides assistance to members of the media interested in contacting researchers, staff, or administrators. We can arrange interviews and photography sessions, provide background information and arrange for temporary parking permits to assist you in accessing campus and Ames Laboratory facilities. You can find us on Twitter at @Ames NatLab and on Facebook at https://www.facebook.com/AmesNationalLab/ .

entry.title: Glossary
entry.summary: 2D Materials : Also called “single-layer materials,” 2D materials are crystalline solids that are one to just a few atoms thick. They tend to be extremely strong, lightweight, flexible, and good conductors of heat and electricity. Graphene is the most well-known 2D material.

entry.title: Ames National Laboratory to contribute to AI-Driven magnet manufacturing project under DOE Genesis Mission
entry.summary: Ames National Laboratory to contribute to AI-Driven magnet manufacturing project under DOE Genesis Mission

entry.title: Ames Lab scientist provides AI-driven roadmap for future permanent magnet design
entry.summary: Researchers at Ames National Laboratory are advancing the discovery of materials for rare-earth-free permanent magnets by combining fundamental physics with artificial intelligence. The work contributes to the U. S. Department of Energy’s (DOE) Genesis Mission , which unites DOE National Labs, industry, and academia to harness artificial intelligence (AI) for scientific breakthroughs in energy, discovery science, and national security, including a focus on securing America’s supply of rare earths and other critical minerals.

entry.title: Ames Lab, CMI Hub partner in NSF Critical Materials Crossroads Engine
entry.summary: Ames Lab, CMI Hub partner in NSF Critical Materials Crossroads Engine

entry.title: AI-powered hunt for stronger magnets earns Iowa State and Ames Lab $2.7 million grant
entry.summary: AI-powered hunt for stronger magnets earns Iowa State and Ames Lab $2.7 million grant

entry.title: New equipment expands DNP-NMR research capabilities at Ames Lab
entry.summary: Researchers at the U. S. Department of Energy Ames National Laboratory are advancing materials research with the installation of a new high-field Dynamic Nuclear Polarization Nuclear Magnetic Resonance (DNP-NMR) spectrometer. The system strengthens the lab’s DNP-NMR research capabilities by providing a much higher resolution for solid-state NMR experiments. The field strength of the new material is 14.1 T, which is 50% higher than that from the previously-installed DNP instrument.

entry.title: SRNL’s Wellman joins Critical Materials Innovation Hub bringing lab’s expertise and capabilities
entry.summary: Senior research fellow Dawn Wellman has been appointed to the Critical Materials Innovation Hub executive team, strengthening Savannah River National Laboratory’s expertise and capabilities in critical minerals and materials.
